response: Japan, home to a population of 125 million, has a GDP of $4.2 trillion with a modest growth rate of 1.1%, while maintaining a low unemployment rate of 2.6% and an inflation rate of 3.2%. The country carries a notably high debt-to-GDP ratio of 263%, yet boasts one of the world's longest life expectancies at 84.6 years and strong exports totaling $756 billion.
